seo.title: Claude Tool Use Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for Claude Tool Use agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams leveraging Claude's tool use capabilities for tasks like patient triage, diagnostic support, and records management face immense risks due to the sensitivity of PHI and strict regulations like HIPAA. Cascading errors from tool chains or inconsistent reasoning can lead to misdiagnoses or data breaches, resulting in patient harm and fines up to $1.5M per violation. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify tool outputs, ensure auditability, and prevent unintended actions in high-stakes environments.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects PHI from unauthorized disclosure; requires de-identification before processing and audit trails for all access.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies tool outputs for PHI leakage, enforces de-identification checks, and logs immutable audit trails for every Claude tool call.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records and signatures be trustworthy, reliable, and equivalent to paper records with validation of systems.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex wraps Claude's messages.create() with verify() to validate tool chain integrity and produce compliant audit logs for AI decisions.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notifications and increased penalties; requires secure handling of PHI in digital systems.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects and blocks PHI exfiltration in tool responses, ensuring patient data stays within compliant environments.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated controls for security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy over time.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides continuous monitoring and verification of Claude agent behaviors, generating SOC 2-compliant evidence of control effectiveness.

industryRisks.name: PHI Leakage in Tool Outputs
industryRisks.description: Claude tools querying databases return unredacted patient data in responses.
industryRisks.example: A diagnostic tool pulls full SSN and medical history, exposing PHI to downstream systems.

industryRisks.name: Cascading Tool Chain Errors
industryRisks.description: Early tool failure (e.g., wrong patient ID) propagates through chain, leading to incorrect treatment recommendations.
industryRisks.example: Patient lookup fails, but downstream dosing calculator proceeds with default values, risking overdose.

industryRisks.name: Conflicting Parallel Tool Results
industryRisks.description: Multiple tools (labs, imaging) return inconsistent vitals without resolution, confusing clinician.
industryRisks.example: Lab tool reports normal glucose; imaging tool flags diabetic crisis—no reconciliation provided.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent Reasoning in Extended Thinking
industryRisks.description: Claude's internal reasoning contradicts final tool-based output, eroding trust in AI decisions.
industryRisks.example: Thinks 'patient stable' internally but tools recommend emergency intervention without explanation.

implementation.title: Verify Tool Chain for PHI Safety
implementation.description: Wrap Claude's tool use response with Vex to check for PHI leakage and tool consistency before clinical use.
implementation.code:
import anthropic
import vex

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-key")

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    max_tokens=1024,
    tools=[{"name": "get_patient_record", "input_schema": {...}}],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Analyze patient 12345 vitals"}]
)

verified = vex_client.verify(
    span_id="healthcare-tool-call-1",
    inputs=message.input,
    outputs=message.content,
    policy="healthcare-phi-no-leak"
)
if verified:
    print("Safe for clinical use:", message.content[0].text)

implementation.title: Audit Parallel Tool Calls
implementation.description: Resolve conflicts from parallel tools (e.g., labs + imaging) with Vex consistency checks.
implementation.code:
import anthropic
import vex

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
vex_client = vex.Client()

response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-opus",
    tools=[
        {"name": "query_labs"},
        {"name": "query_imaging"}
    ],
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Check vitals for patient 67890"}]
)

result = vex_client.verify(
    span_id="parallel-tools-audit",
    inputs=response.input,
    outputs=[tool.text for tool in response.content if tool.type == "tool_use"],
    policy="resolve-conflicts",
    framework="claude-tool-use"
)
if result.passed:
    print("Resolved vitals:", result.output_summary)

implementation.title: TypeScript: Guard Computer Use Agent
implementation.description: Prevent unintended screen actions in EHR systems by verifying Claude computer use outputs.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Claude Tool Use in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
A mid-sized hospital deploys Claude with tool use for real-time patient triage in the ER. The agent chains tools: first querying de-identified EHR records, then parallel calls to lab results and imaging analysis, followed by a dosing calculator for medication recommendations. During a busy shift, the system processes 50+ patients hourly, relying on Claude to flag critical cases and suggest interventions. Without guardrails, early tool failures (like misfetched patient IDs) cascade, producing inconsistent outputs that overburden clinicians.

In one incident, parallel tools return conflicting glucose readings (normal vs. critical) without resolution, and the agent’s extended thinking notes stability while final output recommends insulin—leading to a near-miss overdose. PHI briefly appears in logs due to incomplete de-identification, risking HIPAA violation.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex, every messages.create() tool response is wrapped with vex.verify(), enforcing PHI checks, tool consistency, and reasoning alignment. Cascading errors are caught early (99.7% reduction), conflicts auto-resolved with auditable explanations, and unintended EHR actions blocked. The hospital achieved SOC 2 Type II certification, reduced triage errors by 87%, and avoided $2.1M in potential fines. Clinicians report 40% faster decision-making with full audit trails for every AI-assisted call.
